title: Enable CORS Headers via HAProxy Annotation
description:
## Enable CORS Headers via HAProxy Annotation

Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) headers must be present on API responses for browser-based clients on a different origin. HAProxy Ingress can inject these headers without modifying the backend application.

**Your task:**

An `Ingress` named `api-ingress` exists in the `haproxy-lab` namespace. Annotate it to enable CORS with the following settings:
- `haproxy.org/cors-enable: 'true'`
- `haproxy.org/cors-allow-origin: '*'`
- `haproxy.org/cors-allow-methods: 'GET, POST, OPTIONS'`

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl get ingress api-ingress -n haproxy-lab -o jsonpath='{.metadata.annotations.haproxy\.org/cors-enable}'
```

hints.title: Annotating with kubectl annotate
hints.body: Use kubectl annotate to add all three CORS annotations in one command.
hints.command:
kubectl annotate ingress api-ingress -n haproxy-lab \
  haproxy.org/cors-enable='true' \
  haproxy.org/cors-allow-origin='*' \
  haproxy.org/cors-allow-methods='GET, POST, OPTIONS'

setup_commands.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: api-ingress
  namespace: haproxy-lab
spec:
  ingressClassName: haproxy
  rules:
  - http:
      paths:
      - path: /api
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: api-svc
            port:
              number: 80
EOF

validation.description: Check that the three CORS annotations are set on api-ingress
